_description: Benchmark questions for hybrid-vs-keyword evaluation. Each question tagged by retrieval_type so you can prove the graph earns its place. Ground-truth answers included for scoring.

_tags.keyword_answerable: A single doc contains the answer; keyword/vector search alone can find it.
_tags.graph_only: Answer requires traversing relationships across multiple docs; keyword search returns disconnected fragments.
_tags.hybrid: Needs both semantic lookup and relationship reasoning.

questions.question: Was there any early warning before the Pump P-204 failure, and what should have been done according to procedure?
questions.ground_truth: Yes. Elevated DE bearing vibration was flagged 21 days before failure in inspection IR-556 (4.6 mm/s, Zone C). Maintenance log ML-1183 topped up lube but DEFERRED bearing replacement due to no spare, against M-118 clause 4.3 which requires replacement within 14 days. Vibration crossed the 7.1 mm/s Zone D alarm 5 days before failure (VS-204) with no work order raised. Incident INC-2024-07 was the resulting bearing seizure. Procedure M-118 (per OISD-132) required replacement, not deferral.
questions.why_keyword_fails: The five facts live in five separate documents with no shared keyword. Keyword search on 'P-204 failure' returns the incident report only, missing the warning chain.

questions.question: Which regulation governs bearing replacement timelines for Pump P-204?
questions.ground_truth: OISD-132. Pump P-204 maintenance follows Procedure M-118, which implements OISD-132 (Inspection of Rotating Equipment). Requires the equipment->procedure->regulation hop.
questions.why_keyword_fails: No single document says 'P-204 is governed by OISD-132'. The link is P-204 -> M-118 -> OISD-132 across documents.

questions.question: What is the vibration alarm threshold for Pump P-204's DE bearing?
questions.ground_truth: 7.1 mm/s RMS (ISO 10816-3 Zone D). Stated directly in VS-204 and IR-556.
questions.why_keyword_fails: N/A - keyword search handles this fine. Included as a control to show hybrid does not lose easy lookups.

questions.question: What lubricant grade is specified for feed water pumps?
questions.ground_truth: ISO VG 68, relubrication interval 1500 operating hours. Stated in LS-07.

questions.question: Who inspected Pump P-204 and were they qualified?
questions.ground_truth: R. Krishnan, a certified vibration analyst (ISO 18436-2 Cat II). From IR-556.
questions.why_keyword_fails: Mostly keyword-findable, but connecting 'inspector' to 'qualification' benefits from entity linking.

questions.question: Did the motor driving P-204 have any problems around the same time?
questions.ground_truth: No. Motor M-204 (which drives P-204) was inspected in IR-560 and was nominal - all readings normal. The problem was the driven pump P-204's bearing, not the motor. Requires distinguishing the motor from the pump it drives.
questions.why_keyword_fails: Keyword search on 'P-204 problem' may wrongly surface the motor doc or conflate motor and pump. Graph keeps them as distinct linked entities.

questions.question: Why was the bearing replacement on P-204 delayed?
questions.ground_truth: The spare bearing (SKF 6316 C3) was not in stores at the time of work order ML-1183, so replacement was deferred to next planned shutdown and lube top-up applied as interim. Approved by V. Menon.
questions.why_keyword_fails: Findable in one doc, but the significance (that this violated M-118) needs the graph link to the procedure.

questions.question: Was the Zone D vibration alarm on P-204 acted upon?
questions.ground_truth: No. VS-204 shows the alarm crossed 7.1 mm/s five days before failure. Shift handover SH-0717 noted the alarm but no work order was raised. The failure INC-2024-07 followed. M-118 clause 4.4 required an immediate work order.
questions.why_keyword_fails: The 'alarm raised but not acted on' story spans the sensor log, the handover log, and the incident - three docs, no shared keyword.
